data.name: EnableInterviewerAccountLockoutHelpText
data.value:
When enabled, interviewers accounts will be locked out and these users will not be able to log into the system if the number of consecutive failed log in attempts matches the amount specified. When an account is locked out an email will be sent to the address(es) specified in “Email address for CATI notifications”.
Note: Even when disabled, interviewer accounts will still be locked out based on the default maximum number of failed login attempts. Usually the default value is 100 although this can be changed by the system administrator.

data.name: SupervisorEmailAddressHelpText
data.value: The email address(es) specified will receive an email whenever a system event occurs, such as an interviewer account is locked out or notification of CATI Clean-Up.

data.name: StatePriorityHelpText
data.value:
The default priority value is assigned to calls when the "Move" or "Move and Reschedule" operation is applied in Call Management. For instance, if the default priority for the "Busy" status is 5, any call moved and rescheduled to the "Busy" status will have its priority set to 5, regardless of its previous status.
Changing the default priority value will not affect the priorities of existing calls.

data.name: Start/continue interview text
data.value:
To start your interview, simply click "Start interview" below.
To continue suspended interview, supply the password, then click "Start interview".

data.value:
Are you sure you want to delete the selected user(s)? This action is final and cannot be undone.
Please confirm by typing "1234" below. 

Deleting an interviewer's account is not recommended as it can affect historical productivity reports, since the system will no longer track the deleted user's work. Instead, consider these alternatives: 
 1.Lock the user to prevent system access (this action is reversible). 
 2.Rename the user.

data.name: EmptyConfirmitGuestLogin
data.value:
An authorization error has occured.
This problem may be caused by missing or empty value for the "guest" login in CP configuration file.
It can be fixed by adding proper key with valid non-empty value into web.config of CP like this:
<configuration>
   <appSettings>
      <add key="ConfirmitGuestLogin" value="valid_login"/>
   </appSettings>
</configuration>

data.name: CallAttemptsReportDescription
data.value: The is a log of all call attempts made by interviewers in the survey(s) selected.

data.name: ExportCallHistoryDataHelp
data.value:
The zipped file contains a tab delimited .txt file containing call activity for the selected surveys during the selected period, the data is sorted first by survey ID then date. The order of the columns in the file is:

Date time – UTC timestamp for the time the call finished
Survey ID – The Survey ID, or “BREAK-Survey ID” for survey-specific breaks, or “BREAK” for non-survey-specific breaks, or “LOGIN” and “LOGOUT” for session events
Survey Name – The Name of the survey
Interview ID – The interview ID (respondent ID) for the call attempt made
Interviewer ID – The numeric ID of the CATI interviewer that made the call
Interviewer Name – The name of the CATI interviewer that made the call
Telephone Number – The telephone number of the respondent
Extended Status – The numeric value of the extended status for the call outcome
Duration – The duration of the call made in seconds
Waiting time – The time spent (in seconds) waiting for the call to be delivered

data.name: ExportQuotaStatusReportHelp
data.value:
For each quota defined in the survey that is available in the CATI supervisor, the following information will be included:

Quota cell label – The name of the quota cell, constructed from the answers used in the quota definition
Limit – The limit set for this quota cell
Counter – The number of completed interviews for this quota cell
Counter Percentage – The percentage of completed interviews for this quota cell
Remaining – The number of interviews needed to meet the limit for this quota cell
Call counts – The number of interviews that have calls in the CATI system for this quota cell
Used calls – The number of interviews that do not have calls in the system for this quota cell, excluding completes e.g. Refusals etc…
Burn rate – The rate at which sample is being processed for this quota cell (Used Calls divided by Counter)

data.name: PersonImportHelp
data.value:
The XLS file can optionally contain the follow columns in the first row, used to automatically map the fields:

Group - Comma separated list of groups the interviewer should be a member of
Login - Login username for the interviewer
Password - Password for the interviewer
Person description - Description of the interviewer
Choice - Interview task choice for the interviewer (1 = Automatic, 2 = Manual, 3 = Survey, 4 = Choice)
Location - Interviewer location
AutomaticSurvey - Automatic survey ID (ignored if task choice is not 'Survey Selection')

Alternatively, manual mapping can be performed by right clicking on the column after clicking OK.

Duplicate numbers or numbers containing spaces or special characters such as ( ) + # - will be rejected. The * character can be used as a wildcard at the end of a number. For example, specifying 1234* will prevent calls to all numbers beginning with 1234.

data.name: help_BvTCI_MaxRings

data.name: help_BvTCI_TelephoneNumberPrefix
data.value: Supply a string that should be added to the start of every telephone number before it is dialed by the dialer. This prefix will not update the telephone number in the survey data.

data.name: help_PROTS_AbandonmentRate
data.value:
The value supplied controls the abandonment rate when dialing in full predictive mode, this parameter determines the rate by which connected calls would be dropped because an interviewer is not available. Valid values and their meaning are shown below:
Value Resulting Abandonment Rate
01 in 10,000 (.01%)
55 in 1,000 (.5%)
1010 in 1,000 (1%)
2020 in 1,000 (2%)
3030 in 1,000 (3%)
4040 in 1,000 (4%)
5050 in 1,000 (5%)
6060 in 1,000 (6%)
7070 in 1,000 (7%)
8080 in 1,000 (8%)
9090 in 1,000 (9%)
100100 in 1,000 (10%)

data.name: help_PROTS_AnsMachineDetect

Warning: Enabling answer phone detection can lead to a short delay before voice is delivered to interviewers for all call attempts.

data.name: HelpQuotaForClustering
data.value: Select a quota from the list of quotas available. Only quotas that have the “Display Quota in CATI Supervisor” setting enabled in Authoring are available for selection. When quota clustering is enabled, the number of active calls being interviewed at any one time in a particular quota cell will be limited to the threshold value specified.Quota clustering only applies to interviewers operating in survey assigned mode; additionally it is not applicable to predictive dialing nor the use of call groups. When quota clustering is disabled, the active cluster cell counts are reset.

data.name: HelpQuotaForBalancing
data.value: Select a quota from the list of quotas available. Only quotas that have the “CATI Delivery When Quota not Full” setting enabled in Authoring are available for selection. When quota balancing is enabled, if cells fall below the specified threshold then these cells will be considered as “in need of promotion”. Promotion is achieved by increasing the priority of selected calls. When quota balancing is performed, the selected calls will have their call priorities increased to the specified priority value. The number of calls promoted is determined dynamically, based on the current dials per hour and strike rate for this survey. If manual call editing or call activation is performed on any calls that are currently promoted, their promotion will be revoked as part of the editing or activation process.

data.name: HelpClusterQuotaThreshold
data.value: The threshold value is a configurable absolute figure. It is the maximum number of active calls being interviewed at any one time in a particular quota cell for every cell in the quota cluster.

data.name: HelpThreshold
data.value: The threshold value is a configurable percentage figure. If the difference between the “quota achieved” figure for the most populated quota cell and any other cells falls below this threshold, the quota balancing promotion procedure will be initiated.

data.name: HelpPriority
data.value: The priority value entered will be the priority value to which activated calls’ priorities are changed when quota balancing promotes calls. If a low value is entered here, existing calls may already have higher priority values, resulting in quota balancing effectively not promoting calls. Care should be taken not to exceed appointment priority values (typically 1000) as this may result in appointments not being delivered as expected.

data.name: HelpFilter
data.value:
It is possible to define the filter used to specify which respondent data records are to be selected for promotion using quota balancing. This filter must have registered all the questions that make up the quota; if some question data is missing then these records will not be selected for promotion. For example:

Quota is based on gender and age.

Gender is preloaded as respondent data.

Age is unknown.

If both gender and age are selected, no calls will be promoted due to the fact that the age data is unavailable. In this scenario, if only gender is selected, promotion can at least be effective based on the respondent gender only.

data.name: CallHistoryReportHelpText
data.value: When enabled, a zipped tab-delimited file will be emailed to the specified email address(es) at, or soon after, the time specified every day. The file will contain call attempt data for the previous 24hr period, in the same format as the "Call History Export" from the survey list, for all surveys, and will include break times and login, logout events. Multiple email addresses can be supplied and should be separated using a ;

data.name: InterviewerProductivityReportHelpText
data.value: When enabled, an exported PDF of the Interviewer productivity report will be emailed to the specified email address(es) at, or soon after, the time specified every day. The PDF will contain productivity data for the previous 24hr period for all surveys that have been worked on, and data will be exported for all interviewers who have worked, including dialer attempts where applicable. Break times will not be included in calculations. Multiple email addresses can be supplied and should be separated using a ;

data.name: SurveyOverviewReportHelpText
data.value: When enabled, an exported PDF of the Survey overview report will be emailed to the specified email address(es) at, or soon after, the time specified every day. The PDF will contain productivity data for the previous 24hr period for all surveys that have been worked on, and data will be exported for all interviewers who have worked, including dialer attempts where applicable. Multiple email addresses can be supplied and should be separated using a ;

data.name: SurveyProductivityReportHelpText
data.value: When enabled, an exported PDF of the Survey productivity report will be emailed to the specified email address(es) at, or soon after, the time specified every day. The PDF will contain productivity data for the previous 24hr period for all surveys that have been worked on, and data will be exported for all interviewers who have worked. Multiple email addresses can be supplied and should be separated using a ;

data.name: ExportQuotaStatusReportHintText
data.value: Click "Generate report" to begin the download of a zipped tab-delimited file containing the current quota information for the selected survey.

data.name: PersonViewCallGroupsHelpText
data.value: For an interviewer to be delivered calls automatically for a survey where Call Groups are enabled the interviewer needs to be a member of a Call Group. If they are not a member of a Call Group, no calls will be delivered automatically. An interviewer can be a member of one Call Group only, this can be changed at any time and takes effect on the next call delivered. Interviewers that are working as a member of a call group need to have the “Survey Selection” task choice otherwise Call Groups cannot be used. When working as a member of a Call Group, calls are delivered based upon the Extended Statuses in that Call Group.

data.name: SuveyViewCallGroupsHelpText
data.value: When Call Groups are enabled for a survey, only interviewers that are assigned to a call group can receive calls automatically for this survey, manual interviewers can still work though. Call Groups can be enabled to deliver calls to interviewers based on the Extended Statuses assigned to the Call Group that they are a member of. Calls are delivered in priority order, call priority first, then Call Group priority, when several Extended Status codes have the same Call Group priority they are delivered sequentially based on all interviewers working in that Call Group. If a call is assigned to an interviewer group it will not be delivered when Call Groups are enabled for a survey. Call Groups are not supported for surveys with the predictive dial mode enabled.

data.name: FcdBehavior
data.value: Call filtering behaviour for filled quotas

data.name: FcdBehaviorHelpText
data.value:
Change status and delete calls
When enabled, CATI interviews will automatically be removed from the CATI call list and be given an extended status value of 27 (Filtered by call delivery)

Disable calls with re-enabling on open cell(s)
When enabled, CATI interviews will be set as disabled and will remain in the Scheduled list. Properties of the call will also be maintained, such as ‘Time to call’, ‘Priority’, ‘Shift’ and ‘Assignment’. If a quota cell is re-opened the interviews will be re-enabled automatically.

data.name: DefaultCallDeliveryModeHelpText
data.value:
This setting specifies the default call delivery mode that will be applied to all newly launched surveys. This setting can be overridden at the survey level by adjusting the mode setting on the general settings tab for the survey.

Order by ID (lowest first)
When enabled, CATI interviews will be delivered to interviewers in the order with which they are found in the scheduled calls list (lowest ID first) unless some calls have been given a higher priority.

Random order
When enabled, CATI interviews will be delivered in a random order, unless some calls have been given a higher priority.

data.name: EnablePasswordExpirationHelpText

data.name: UserForEncryptionHelpText
data.value: When encryption is enabled, any scheduled email exports with attachments that may contain personally identifiable respondent information (e.g Call History) will be encrypted using the key of the user account specified. The account should be a professional account within the same company and have a PGP key registered within the profile.

data.name: AlwaysEncryptFilesHelpText
data.value: Check this box to encrypt all data exports that may contain personally identifiable information. The setting enforces all applicable exports to be encrypted, it is applied for all users within the company who have access to the CATI supervisor UI. Only users with pre-configured private PGP encryption keys and software will be able to decrypt the exported files. Please refer to the user manual for further information.

Note: In preview mode calls, the button will only become active after the dial has been executed.

data.name: EnableLogoutFromErrorAndWaitingScreen

data.name: EnableLogoutFromErrorAndWaitingScreenHelpText
data.value:
When enabled, interviewers that encounter errors or long delays for an interview will be provided with an option to logout. If the interviewer uses the logout option their current session will be terminated and they will need to log back in to resume working.

Note: This functionality is enabled by default and is only applicable for interviewers working with the browser based interviewer console.

data.name: MarkNewsRead
data.value: I have read this and do not wish to have it displayed again

data.name: EndOfShiftThresholdHelpText
data.value:
Set end of shift threshold
Time at the end of a shift which controls call delivery algorithm to favour calls for a time zone in which a shift is about to be finished

Example
There are 2 active time zones where one of them is ahead of the other by one hour. Threshold is set for 30 minutes. If interviewer requests an interview at the time which in less than 30 minutes before the end of a shift in a first time zone (which is ahead) then interview for first time zone will be delivered first (we assume that calls priorities are equal). If threshold is not set then interview will be delivered based on either lower interview id or randomly based on corresponding survey property irrespective of time zone is set for interview record (again we assume calls priorities are equal).

data.name: TimezonesBalancingSettingsHint
data.value: Below are time zones prioritization settings

data.name: SuveyTargetHelpText

data.name: DdiNumbersRecordedMessagesCampaignIsNotAvailableMessage
data.value: Message to play if the survey is not available

data.name: DdiNumbersRecordedMessagesFirstColumnTitle
data.value: Case to play a recorded message

data.name: DdiNumbersRecordedMessagesIncomingCallCompulsoryMessage
data.value: Compulsory message: A message which (if defined) must be played in full to the respondent as soon as the inbound call is connected

data.name: DdiNumbersRecordedMessagesIncomingCallMessage
data.value: Waiting audio: A message (or music) that will be played to the respondent whilst waiting to connect an available interviewer

data.name: DdiNumbersRecordedMessagesSecondColumnTitle
data.value: Specify the full URL of the associated audio file

data.name: DdiNumbersRecordedMessagesSystemFaultMessage
data.value: Message to play to respondent if system needs to drop call because of an unexpected error

data.name: DdiNumbersDefaultRecordedMessagesHint
data.value: The default recorded messages will be applied for all inbound surveys (unless overrides are defined for DDI specific messages)

data.name: DdiNumbersIncomingCallCompulsoryMessageHelpText
data.value:
If this message is supplied and set to play once then it will be played in full to the respondent as soon as the call is connected (the respondent will have no way to interrupt/skip the message). After the message has finished playing the system will start to play the 'Waiting audio' (a message or music) which may continue to play until an agent is ready to accept the call (at which time the On hold message will be interrupted).

Example message:
"Please note that the interview may be recorded for our quality control process."

data.name: DdiNumbersIncomingCallHelpText
data.value: The supplied audio will be played as soon as the inbound call is connected to the system and whilst the respondent is held for an available interviewer. If a compulsory message is defined then the waiting audio will only start after this message has first been played. Waiting audio will be interrupted automatically as soon as an interviewer is connected to the call.

data.name: DdiNumbersCampaignIsNotAvailableHelpText
data.value:
The system will play this message if…
- The survey is closed
- The survey cannot be found
- The survey was deleted

Example message:
"Unfortunately this survey is no longer available."

data.name: DdiNumbersDropCallInterviewNotFoundHelpText
data.value:
By default when the inbound caller has an unknown or unspecified caller ID a new interview will be started in a locating survey so that an available agent can be connected and then ask some questions to locate the appropriate survey and record. However if this functionality is not enabled the system will simply play this message for any case where the caller ID cannot be found and matched to the applicable survey and interview record (this includes the case where the caller ID is withheld/empty).

Example message:
"Sorry, your caller ID number has not been recognized, please check and try again."

data.name: DdiNumbersDropCallOutOfShiftHelpText
data.value:
The system will play this message if a caller ID is recognized but the call cannot be answered because the call center is closed at the time of the call. The availability status for the call center is based upon the permitted shift times (as defined in the schedule/shifts tab for the applicable survey schedule) the current time for the call center is based on the default time zone setting (as specified under Resources/Active Timezone list).

Example message:
"Unfortunately our call center is currently closed, operating hours are between 9am and 9pm Monday to Friday, please try again later."

data.name: DdiNumbersSystemFaultHelpMessage
data.value:
The system will play this message if the call needs to be dropped due to an unexpected fault.

Example message:
"There has been a fault while attempting to service your call, please try again later."

data.name: ReconnectAutomaticallyHelpText
data.value: Specifies the amount of time the system should continue to attempt to restore a dropped dialer connection.

data.name: InterviewerLocationHelpText
data.value: The location field is an optional property that can be used to associate the interviewer with a localised dialer gateway. Please note that only valid location names (as specified in the dialer gateway configuration settings) should be entered here.

data.name: IvrStaticSettingsTransferTimeoutHelpText
data.value:
The timeout value (specified in seconds) determines how long the system will wait when attempting to transfer from IVR to a live agent. If the transfer to a live agent is not completed within the given timeout period it will be returned to IVR.

The value supplied must be a positive number.

data.name: AllowMonitoringBargingMode
data.value: Allow the use of call barging during live monitoring

data.name: AllowMonitoringCoachingMode
data.value: Allow the use of interviewer coaching during live monitoring

data.name: AllowMonitoringBargingModeHelpText
data.value: Allow the use of call barging during live monitoring. The Supervisor can talk to both the interviewer and the respondent whilst monitoring the interviewer.

data.name: AllowMonitoringCoachingModeHelpText
data.value: Allow the use of interviewer coaching mode during live monitoring. The Supervisor can whisper instructions to the interviewer whilst monitoring them and can hear the audio of both the interviewer and the respondent. The respondent cannot hear the Supervisor.

data.name: MonitoringConsoleToolbarButtonsHintText
data.value: Enabling the settings below will activate the corresponding buttons on the monitoring console

data.name: CreateOnlyCliType
data.value: Do not match CLI (always create new records)

data.name: InboundBehaviorHelpText
data.value:
This setting specifies how the system will behave when receiving an inbound call. There are three possible behavior types:
1. Attempt to match inbound CLI with existing sample records (do not create new records when no matches found)
2. Attempt to match inbound CLI with existing sample records (create a new record if no match found)
3. Do not attempt to match inbound CLI with existing sample records (always create a new record)
Note: A 'CLI' is the number of the inbound caller (Caller ID). The system will attempt to match this against values contained in the 'TelephoneNumber' field.

data.name: Error_NoPermissionForCopyToDefaultAction
data.value: This operation can only be performed by a CATI administrator, please contact your system administrator or Confirmit support if you need assistance in performing this operation.

data.name: SurveyHistoryLoopHint
data.value: Call history loop information shows the data written to all variables defined in a survey based loop with the reserved name 'callhistoryinfo'. If the survey does not contain a loop named 'callhistoryinfo' nothing will be displayed here. Data will not be written to the callhistoryinfo loop for predictively dialed call attempts where outcomes (like 'busy' or 'no reply') are automatically disposed of (unless custom actions are defined in the survey schedule).

data.name: InterviewerProductivityReportTemplateSelectionHelp
data.value: The Interviewer productivity will be built in accordance with the specified template. A new template can be created by selecting the ‘Manage customizations’ button on the Interviewer productivity report screen.

data.name: RemoveAdvancedFilterQuestion

data.name: InterviewDurationTooltip
data.value: Supply interview duration filter in seconds (display in mm:ss)

data.name: PleaseFillCorrectRedAlert
data.value: Red alert must be greater than yellow alert

data.name: BreakTypeRedAlertHelpText
data.value: The red alert will cause the cell of the ‘Duration’ column of the ‘Interviewers List’ Activity View to turn red at the specified number of minutes after interviewer goes to this type of break. The alert will also override the alert settings specified in the Activity View

data.name: BreakTypeYellowAlertHelpText
data.value: The yellow alert will cause the cell of the ‘Duration’ column of the ‘Interviewers List’ Activity View to turn yellow at the specified number of minutes after interviewer goes to this type of break. The alert will also override the alert settings specified in the Activity View

data.name: EditCallPropertiesOfEntireList
data.value: Edit Call Properties of Entire List

data.name: EditCallPropertiesOfOneItem
data.value: Edit Call Properties of 1 Selected Item

data.name: EditCallPropertiesOfSelectedItems
data.value: Edit Call Properties of {0} Selected Items

data.name: EditCallsGeneralInfo
data.value: Select which shared properties you would like to update:

data.name: EditExtendedStatusHelpText
data.value: Changing the extended status with this setting will only move the selected calls into the chosen status. This operation will be performed without running the survey schedule and applying applicable actions that might be defined within it.

data.name: EditCallsGeneralInfoForOneCall
data.value: Select which properties you would like to update:

data.name: EditCallsNoSelectedToggles
data.value: At least one toggle should be enabled

data.name: EditCallsTimeToCallIsBiggerThenTimeToExpire
data.value: The Time to Call is past the Time to Expire.

data.name: EditTimeToCallHelpText
data.value: The ‘Time to Call’ value should be supplied using the time in the respondent time zone

data.name: EditTimeToExpireHelpText
data.value: The ‘Time to Expire’ value should be supplied using the time in the respondent time zone

data.name: ItIsNecessaryToReassignCalls
data.value: Important Notice.\nThere are currently calls that are assigned to a shift type other than ‘any valid’ or ‘none’, after applying a new schedule it is now necessary to manually re-assign all calls to appropriate shift types. This can be achieved by using the ‘Change shift type’ operation in the Call Management interface.

data.name: SampleUpdateHelpViewer
data.value: When this setting is enabled the rule will only be executed when respondent sample records are being updated. The rule will not be executed in any other scenarios and so it should only be enabled if this specific purpose is applicable.

data.name: cnfr_DeleteCallAttempt
data.value: Are you sure you want to delete the selected call attempt? Please note that this is not reversible. After deletion this call attempt will no longer be included in the Call History or productivity reports.

data.name: cnfr_EditCallAttempt
data.value: Are you sure you want to edit the selected call attempt?

data.name: DisplayTimeTooltip
data.value: The difference in seconds between the time the system finds and/or connects the call and the time the page is loaded into the console.

data.name: PreviewTimeTooltip
data.value: The difference in seconds between the interview start time and the first time the phone connection was established within the interview. Only applicable for Preview interviews.

data.name: ConnectedTimeTooltip
data.value: The difference in seconds between the first time the phone connection was established within the interview and the last time the phone connection was disconnected within the interview.

data.name: WrapTimeTooltip
data.value: The difference in seconds between the last time the phone connection was disconnected within the interview and the start of the open end review (if open end review is enabled for the survey) or the interview end (if open end review is not enabled for the survey).

data.name: DurationTooltip
data.value: The difference between interviewing start time and finish time, which also includes Openend Review time and ring time (if dialing in automatic or preview modes).

data.name: WaitingTimeTooltip
data.value: The time period between first login into the system, completion of a break or end of the previous call and the start of the next scheduled call.

data.name: SurveyScheduleNotLaunched
data.value: The currently applied schedule ‘{0}’ has not been launched, the default ‘{1}’ will be used unless it is launched.

data.name: TimezonesHint
data.value: Activated time zones are those which have been used in past or current surveys (time zones are automatically activated whenever a sample record is imported with a previously unused time zone ID). The default time zone of the current call center is highlighted in bold whilst default time zones for other call centers are highlighted in light gray. The currently applicable time zone bias is highlighted in green. Deprecated time zones (shown with strikethrough) are no longer valid and must not be used.

data.name: ErrorMessageTooLong
data.value: Message is too long ({0} symbols). The maximum allowed length is 1024 symbols.

data.name: ErrorMessageEmpty
data.value: Message you are trying to send is empty.

data.name: HideTelephoneNumber
data.value: Hide phone number on the Interviewer screen (browser UI only)

data.name: EnableTwoWayMessaging
data.value: Allow interviewers and supervisors to perform 2-way messaging

data.name: EnableTwoWayMessagingHelpText
data.value:
The toolbar button for messaging must be enabled to support 2-way messaging.
If the setting is disabled the ability to send 2-way messages from the supervisor UI will also be disabled.

data.name: EnableAutomaticScrollingHelpText
data.value: When enabled the interviewer screen will automatically scroll to the position of the first interactive question on the screen when the screen is loaded.

data.name: EnableSoftphoneIntegrationToolbarHelpText
data.value: When enabled the Interviewer account and sign in process will be synchronised to the browser based softphone client so that when the Interviewer logs in to the Interviewer UI they will simultaneously be logged into the softphone client. This functionality will be enabled by default for all interviewers but this can be disabled by adjusting the corresponding Interviewer property. When enabled, a softphone client button will be available in the Interviewer console to access the softphone client interface.

data.name: ChangeSSOHelpText
data.value: When enabled the Interviewer account and sign in process will be synchronized to the browser based softphone client so that when the Interviewer logs in to the Interviewer UI they will simultaneously be logged into the softphone client and a softphone client button will be available in the Interviewer console to access the softphone client interface.

data.name: CompanyLogoUrlHelpText
data.value: Use this feature to supply a custom image that will replace the Forsta logo displayed in the top left corner of the Interviewer UI. Simply specify a URL for an online hosted image with the recommended dimensions. The image could be uploaded to the File Library for hosting.

data.name: PlaybackInBrowserTooltip
data.value: Not all functionality is currently supported in the browser

data.name: PlaybackInBrowserNotSupported
data.value: Playback with the browser based interface is only supported for interviews made using the browser based interviewer UI. Disable the 'Playback in browser' setting to playback the interview using the desktop monitoring interface.

data.name: DoYouWantCloseSurvey
data.value: Do you really want to close this survey?

data.name: HidePii
data.value: Disable visibility to all PII data

data.name: IncludeOpenEndReviewTimeInInterviewDurations
data.value: Include open end review time in interview durations

data.name: IncludeOpenEndReviewTimeInInterviewDurationsHelpText
data.value: When enabled open end review time will be included in the interview duration time, for all interviews completed after this setting is enabled. This will apply to all areas where the interview duration data is shown or used in calculations such as... - Any productivity reports (which currently include the interview duration time) - Call attempts report - Call History in Call Management - Call History data export - Interview durations when extracted via API

data.name: EnableAppointmentTimeZoneAdjustment
data.value: Allow interviewers to adjust the time zone when creating appointments

data.name: EnableAppointmentTimeZoneAdjustmentHelpText
data.value: When creating an appointment the interviewer specifies 'Time due in respondent’s time zone' in the date and time boxes, ticking this checkbox will enable them to specify the time zone of the respondent to properly calculate the time of the appointment.

data.name: ReviewTimeTooltip
data.value: The difference in seconds between the open end review start time and the end of the interview.

data.name: DialerIdsValidationHelpText
data.value: Specify one, multiple, or zero dialer IDs. Specifying none means the first available dialer from the entire dialer list will be selected. Specifying multiple will instruct the system to select an appropriate dialer from the specified list. Multiple dialer IDs should be separated by spaces.

data.name: Err_WrongDialerId
data.value: Specified dialer id does not exist: "{0}"

data.name: ActivityInterviewersExceededWarning
data.value: Only the first {0} interviewers are currently displayed. You have the option to utilize interviewer/group filtering or apply sorting to reveal the interviewers you require.

data.name: EnableInterviewsRandomization
data.value: Enable interview randomization in the manual selection screen

data.name: EnableInterviewsRandomizationHelpText
data.value: When enabled, interviewers with manual selection mode receive a randomized list of interviews. During the randomization process the system takes top (Console.RandomizationInterviewCount) interview records from the sample and then randomly picks (Console.InterviewsCountShownInManualMode) of them to be shown for selection. If the setting to order interviews by priority is enabled, interview records with higher priority appear in the interviews list with a priority and are placed higher in interviews list than interviews with a lower priority, interviews with the same priority are ordered randomly.

data.name: OrderInterviewsByPriority
data.value: Enable ordering of interviews by priority in the manual selection screen

data.name: OrderInterviewsByPriorityHelpText
data.value: When enabled, interviewers with manual selection mode receive a list of interviews ordered by interview priority.

data.name: ManualCallsInsideShiftOnly
data.value: Show only calls vaild in the current shift in the manual selection screen

data.name: ManualCallsInsideShiftOnlyHelpText
data.value: When enabled, only interviews valid for the current shift and those with a time to call value either now or in the past will populate the manual selection screen. The call list will follow the same filtering criteria applied in automatic call delivery mode

data.name: IsChangeAfterFirstLoginRequired
data.value: Enforce password change on first login

data.name: IsChangeAfterFirstLoginRequiredHelpText
data.value: When the setting is enabled any newly created accounts will be presented with a mandatory password change when they first log in.

data.name: AllowTransferredCallsFromOtherSurveyHelpText
data.value: When enabled, transferred calls may come from any survey, even if the receiving interviewer is logged into another survey

data.name: AllowTransferringHelpText
data.value: When enabled, transferred calls may come from the survey the interviewer is currently working on. If the receiving interviewer is using automatic call delivery mode (task choice) and is not logged into a specific survey, transferred calls are allowed from any non-predictive surveys

data.name: AllowTransferredCallsFromAssignedSurveyHelpText
data.value: When enabled, transferred calls may come from any survey assigned to the groups the interviewer belongs to, even if the receiving interviewer is logged into another survey

data.name: AllowAssignedTransferringHelpText
data.value: When enabled, transferred calls may come from the survey the interviewer is currently working on. If the receiving interviewer is using automatic call delivery mode (task choice) and is not logged into a specific survey, transferred calls are allowed from non-predictive surveys assigned to the groups the interviewer belongs to

data.name: ManualDialTypeSelection
data.value: Allow interviewers to select dial type

data.name: ManualDialTypeSelectionHelpText
data.value: When enabled, interviewers will need to select their prefered dialtype (cellphone or landline).

data.name: CallsAvailableNowToolTip
data.value: When calls available now is selected only calls which are available to dial currently will be shown. Disabled calls, calls set for the future or a shift which is not currently active will be hidden from the list.

data.name: PerformanceMetricsLinkHelpText
data.value: Redirect to the Interviewer Statistics Settings page, where you can configure which performance metrics are visible to interviewers.

data.name: AdministrativeGroupHelpText
data.value: For better predictive dialing performance, use the administrative group setting for groups that organize user assignments without requiring skill-based contact info (e.g., language or refusal specialists). When enabled, these groups can only be assigned to survey projects, not directly to call records. Assignments won’t be sent to the dialer, helping maintain larger interviewer resource pools in predictive dialing.

data.name: AdministrativeGroupWarning
data.value: When saved, all calls assigned to this group will be reassigned. These calls will become available to all interviewers who has access to call's survey. This action is irreversible.

data.name: RespondentVariableToSendToTheDialer
data.value: Respondent variables to send to the dialer

data.name: help_RespondentVariableToSendToTheDialer
data.value: A comma-separated list of respondent variables that are sent to the dialer. If the survey does not contain some variables, they are ignored. This feature will only work with supporting dialers (otherwise the supplied information will be ignored).

data.name: PersonAttributesHint
data.value: Add optional custom details about each interviewer (for example, team name or supervisor). These attributes can be used to organize and filter data in custom interviewer productivity reports built using data synced with SmartHub

data.name: InterviewerDisplayNameHelpText
data.value: An optional name for the interviewer that can be displayed dynamically in the survey introduction by inserting GetCatiInterviewerDisplayName()
